title: No VPI access to signals containing double-underscore that are marked public in configuration file
body:
Signals containing a "__" in the signal name that are marked as public in a configuration file that is passed to verilator on the command line (as opposed to marked public by annotating the verilog source), cannot be accessed using the VPI as the vpi handle cannot be found.

Signals containing a "__" in the signal name that are marked as public in the verilog source can be accessed normally.

Issue is demonstrated by the design example in the attached tarball. Untar, cd to directory and type make. This design is derived from the protect_lib example in the verilator distribution.

In the example, the dut module contains signals accum_q, accum1__q, and accum2__q. accum_q and accum2__q are tagged public_flat_rd in the vpi_signals.vlt configuration file. accum1__q is tagged as public_flat_rd in the verilog source.

The testbench in top.v reads the value of these signals on each posedge of the clock using the hdl_read function which is a C function imported from hdl_verilator.cpp. hdl_read uses the VPI to read the signal value.

When the sim is run, an error is printed to the screen indicating that the handle to accum2__q cannot be found.

[verilator_public_double_underscore.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20981494/verilator_public_double_underscore.tar.gz)
